today.title: Today
today.subtitle: Your daily calorie and macro targets.
today.noProfile: Set up your profile to see your daily targets.
today.shoeHint: Set your shoe size in Settings for a better estimate.
today.overTarget: Protein and fat alone exceed your target by {{over}} kcal — lower them, or carbs hit zero.
today.weighInPrompt: You haven't logged your weight today — add it to keep your target accurate.
today.adaptiveMaintenance: Adjusted from your data: measured ≈ {{measured}} (formula {{formula}}) · {{pct}}% confidence — excludes sport, which is added on top
today.totalBurnHint: Baseline maintenance + today's steps & workouts — your real expenditure, and what the net deficit is measured against.

onboarding.welcomeBody: A few quick questions and we'll calculate your personal daily calorie and macro targets.
onboarding.genderHint: We use this to calculate your calorie needs accurately.
onboarding.summaryBody: Based on what you told us, here's your personalized daily target.

diary.title: Diary
diary.noSaved: No saved foods match — try Open Food Facts or add a custom food.
diary.scanHint: Point the camera at a barcode
diary.photoDisclaimer: AI estimate — approximate. Review before logging.
diary.answersPlaceholder: Answer the questions to refine the estimate…

profile.title: Your calorie needs
profile.subtitle: Enter your details to calculate your daily target.

results.title: Your results
results.floorWarning: Your target was raised to the minimum safe intake of {{floor}} kcal/day to avoid an excessive deficit.
results.learnActivity: What do the activity levels mean?

weight.title: Weight
weight.subtitle: Log your weight daily — the weekly average feeds your calorie target.
weight.empty: No data yet — log your first weight.

workouts.title: Workouts
workouts.subtitle: Routines, live sessions, and progression.
workouts.noRoutines: No routines yet — create one to start faster.
workouts.noData: No sets logged for this exercise yet.
workouts.pickToSee: Pick an exercise to see progression.

sedentary.label: Desk job, mostly sitting
sedentary.desc: Office work with little walking during the day.

lightly_active.label: Sitting + some walking
lightly_active.desc: Mostly seated, but on your feet a fair amount.

moderately_active.label: Mostly standing or walking
moderately_active.desc: On your feet most of the day (e.g. retail, teaching).

heavy.label: Physically demanding job
heavy.desc: Manual work most of the day (e.g. a tradesperson).

very_heavy.label: Very heavy labor
very_heavy.desc: Hard physical labor all day (e.g. construction).

formula.title: Mifflin-St Jeor formula
formula.intro: Your basal metabolic rate (BMR) is the energy your body uses at complete rest. We use the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, today's standard:

terms.weight: 10 × weight (kg) — more body mass burns more energy at rest.
terms.height: 6.25 × height (cm) — taller bodies have more tissue to maintain.
terms.age: − 5 × age — resting metabolism declines slowly with age.
terms.constant: +5 (men) / −161 (women) — a sex-based offset for average body composition.

formula.tdee: We multiply BMR by an occupational activity factor to get your maintenance need (TDEE) — the calories that keep your weight stable.
formula.goal: Your goal then shifts the target: cut = maintenance − 500 kcal, maintain = maintenance, bulk = maintenance + 300 kcal. A safety floor (1500 kcal for men, 1200 for women) prevents an extreme deficit.
formula.excludeTitle: Why exercise is not in the base
formula.exclude: The activity factor reflects your job and daily life only. Deliberate exercise and steps are added on top, per day, in a later phase — so a hard training day raises your budget for that day instead of inflating every day.

activity.title: Activity levels
activity.intro: Pick the option that best matches a typical day at work — not your workouts. Each level multiplies your BMR by the factor shown.

trends.title: Trends
trends.subtitle: Calorie adherence and weight over time.
trends.rateWarning: You're losing weight faster than ~1%/week — consider easing the deficit to protect muscle.
trends.noData: Log some food to see adherence.
trends.noWeight: Log weigh-ins to see your weight trend.
trends.maintenanceHint: Learned from {{days}} days of intake vs. weight change · {{pct}}% confidence. Excludes sport — your steps & workouts are added on top separately.
trends.maintenanceLearning: Keep logging food and weight — after ~2 weeks your real maintenance is measured here and feeds your target.

settings.title: Settings
settings.eatBackHint: Add steps/workout calories to your eating budget
settings.note: Imperial input arrives in a later phase; the engine always computes in metric.
settings.planHint: Used to tailor AI meal plans to products you can actually buy. Optional.
settings.shoeSizeHint: Used by the km → steps calculator
settings.mealSlotsHint: Add your own diary sections (e.g. Pre-Workout) and reorder them. The four default meals can't be removed.
settings.backfillServings: Serving sizes from Open Food Facts
settings.backfillHint: Fetch missing serving sizes for your scanned foods, so suggestions size realistic portions.

suggest.title: Fill remaining calories
suggest.loading: Finding foods to fill your day…
suggest.onTarget: You're on target for today — nothing to fill.
suggest.noCandidates: Log a few foods first so I can suggest from your own list.
suggest.prefsPlaceholder: e.g. vegetarian, I have chicken and rice at home…

plan.title: Generate a day's meal plan
plan.empty: Add a few of your own foods first, then I can plan from them.
plan.prefsPlaceholder: e.g. quick meals, no pork, extra protein…

ai.unavailable: AI features are currently unavailable — showing standard results.

demo.tryHint: No sign-up — a private sandbox with sample data that resets automatically.
demo.banner: Live demo — your changes are private and reset automatically

pantry.title: Pantry
pantry.hint: Foods you have at home. Suggestions and day plans prefer these first.
pantry.empty: Nothing here yet — add foods you have at home, or tap ☆ on a food in the Diary.

shopping.title: Shopping list
shopping.addedToList: Added the plan's missing items to your shopping list.
shopping.empty: Nothing to buy. Generate a day plan and tap 🛒, or add items above.

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Everything the app can do — and how to use it.
features.aiOnlyNote: Needs the AI key — disabled in the live demo.
features.aiOptionalNote: Works rule-based; the smarter AI mode needs the key (off in the demo).

today.name: Today dashboard
today.desc: Your daily calorie budget, macros, eaten vs. left, and your net deficit at a glance.
today.how: Open Today to see your budget and log the day.

steps.name: Step tracking
steps.desc: Log your daily steps; they become calories burned and are added to your budget.
steps.how: On Today, type your step count for the day.

diary.name: Food diary
diary.desc: Log breakfast, lunch, dinner and snacks; search your own foods and the Open Food Facts database.
diary.how: Diary → pick a meal → search and add a food.

barcode.name: Barcode scanner
barcode.desc: Scan a product barcode to look it up and log it instantly.
barcode.how: In the Diary, tap the scan icon and point at a barcode.

photo.name: AI photo estimate
photo.desc: Snap a photo of your meal and let AI estimate its calories and macros.
photo.how: In the Diary, upload a meal photo to get an estimate.

labelScan.name: Scan nutrition label
labelScan.desc: Photograph a Nährwerttabelle and AI reads the per-100 g values into a new food for you to review.
labelScan.how: In the Diary's add-food sheet, tap “Scan label” and take a photo of the nutrition table.

customFood.name: Custom foods
customFood.desc: Create your own foods with calories and macros per 100 g.
customFood.how: In the Diary search, choose “create custom food”.

mealSlots.name: Custom meal slots
mealSlots.desc: Add your own diary sections beyond breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack (e.g. Pre-Workout) and reorder them.
mealSlots.how: In Settings → Meal slots, add, rename, reorder or remove your own meals.

copyDay.name: Copy from another day
copyDay.desc: Pull a whole day, a single meal, or just one food (with its portion) from any past day instead of re-entering it.
copyDay.how: Tap “Copy from…” at the top of the Diary, pick the day, then check what to bring over.

fillRemaining.name: Fill remaining calories
fillRemaining.desc: Get food suggestions that fill the calories you have left for the day.
fillRemaining.how: When you have calories left, tap “fill remaining”.

dayPlan.name: Day meal plan
dayPlan.desc: Generate a whole day's meals from your foods, budget and preferences.
dayPlan.how: Tap “generate day plan”, then add it to your diary.

pantry.name: Pantry
pantry.desc: Track what you have at home; suggestions and plans prefer those items first.
pantry.how: Add the items you have at home in Pantry.

shopping.name: Shopping list
shopping.desc: Build a shopping list with prices and compare it against your weekly food budget.
shopping.how: Add items, or push a day plan's missing items to Shopping.

workouts.name: Workout sessions
workouts.desc: Start a live session (empty or from a routine) and log sets, reps and weight.
workouts.how: Workouts → Start, then check off each set as you go.

routines.name: Routines
routines.desc: Build reusable workout templates so you can start a session in one tap.
routines.how: Workouts → “new routine” to set one up.

exerciseLibrary.name: Exercise library
exerciseLibrary.desc: Browse 300+ exercises filtered by muscle group and equipment.
exerciseLibrary.how: Add an exercise from the picker during a session.

progression.name: Progression
progression.desc: See personal records, estimated 1RM and volume trends per exercise.
progression.how: Pick an exercise under Progression to see your trend.

editWorkout.name: Edit past workouts
editWorkout.desc: Fix a logged set's weight or reps, delete a set, or correct a workout's start/end time after you finished it.
editWorkout.how: Tap the ✎ on any workout in History to edit its time and sets.

weight.name: Weight tracking
weight.desc: Log weigh-ins and see a smoothed trend line that powers your calorie target.

body.name: Body measurements
body.desc: Track waist, chest, hips, arm and thigh over time with trend charts.
body.how: Body → log a measurement, optionally with a note.

trends.name: Trends
trends.desc: Calorie adherence, your weight trend, and a maintenance (TDEE) learned from your data.
trends.how: Open Trends after a couple of weeks of logging.

calculator.name: Calorie calculator
calculator.desc: Work out your daily calorie needs from height, age, weight, activity and goal.

formula.name: BMR formula
formula.desc: Understand the Mifflin-St Jeor formula behind your numbers.

activity.name: Activity levels
activity.desc: See what each activity level means and how it scales your needs.
activity.how: Open Activity levels to pick the one that fits your day.

settings.name: Settings
settings.desc: Language, units, meal-planning preferences, food budget and currency.
settings.how: Settings → adjust language, units and preferences.

demo.name: Live demo
demo.desc: A private, throwaway sandbox seeded with sample data that resets automatically.
demo.how: Open the app with “?demo=1”, or tap “Try the live demo” on the login screen.
